front: Composer: French composer and violinist of African descent born in Guadeloupe; son of a slave and a French colonial planter; also known as 'the god of arms' for his fencing (bested Alexandre Picard)
Composer: rebranded the Concert des Amateurs (under Freemason patronage) as the Concert Olympique; commissioned and conducted the premiere of Haydn's Paris Symphonies
Composer: only surviving opera is L'Amant anonyme; wrote eight symphonies concertantes to showcase his violin skill
Composer: called the 'Black Mozart' — but a 2020 New York Times article criticized this label
Composer: removed as director of a Paris opera company due to racist objections; Marie Antoinette failed to advocate for him as Paris Opera director
Composer: Mozart allegedly (but dubiously) based the villain Monostatos in The Magic Flute on him
Composer: a candidate for having composed the basis for the ballet Les petits riens; subject of a 2023 biopic (Chevalier) inventing a feud with Gluck
